questions.question: A shipping company is considering investing in a new fleet management software. Before any planning begins, what should be the primary objective of the initiation phase?
options: Determine whether the project is worth undertaking and define its objectives
Assign every team member to specific tasks
questions.explanation: The initiation phase determines whether the project should proceed and establishes its purpose, objectives and business justification.

questions.question: Which document normally gives a project its formal authorization to begin?
questions.explanation: The Project Charter formally authorizes the project and gives the project manager authority to use organizational resources.

questions.question: A project manager starts developing a detailed budget before the project has been officially approved. What is the biggest issue with this approach?
options: The project may consume resources before a decision has been made to proceed
Budgets cannot be estimated until the project is completed
The project manager must first recruit suppliers
There is no issue because budgeting always comes first
questions.explanation: Without formal approval, significant planning may waste both time and organizational resources if the project is later rejected.

questions.question: Which of the following is LEAST likely to be included in a Project Charter?
questions.explanation: Detailed schedules are developed later during project planning, not during initiation.

questions.question: A logistics company wants to automate invoice processing. Management estimates annual savings of $600,000 while implementation will cost $900,000. Which additional factor should be evaluated before approving the project?
options: Whether employees prefer the current system
Expected project risks and strategic alignment
The color scheme of the new software
How many meeting rooms are available
questions.explanation: Projects should be evaluated based on financial value, strategic fit and associated risks before approval.

questions.question: A project is approved because it is expected to improve customer satisfaction, reduce operating costs and support the company's long-term strategy. Which concept best describes these reasons?
questions.explanation: The business case explains why the project should be undertaken and the value it is expected to deliver.

questions.question: Which statement best distinguishes project initiation from project planning?
options: Initiation decides whether the project should exist, while planning determines how it will be executed.
Initiation creates the detailed work packages, while planning approves the project.
Initiation only concerns budgeting, while planning focuses on quality.
There is no practical difference between the two phases.
questions.explanation: Initiation focuses on project justification and authorization, whereas planning develops the roadmap for execution.

questions.question: A company starts several projects without evaluating whether they support its overall strategy. What is the most likely consequence?
options: Projects may succeed individually but provide little organizational value.
questions.explanation: Projects should contribute to organizational strategy. Otherwise, resources may be spent on initiatives that deliver limited business value.
